meta.setting: Jefferson State frontier, opening around French Gulch, Clear Creek, Shasta, and the Klamath mountains
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western: horseback travel, camp life, hunting, debt, law pressure, outlaw choice, and a hard 1899 frontier tone

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Roads, Clear Creek mining, regional towns, and work patterns for playable map anchors.

research_grounding.title: NPS The Wintu
research_grounding.use: Clear Creek Wintu homeland and land-use context that should shape map generation respectfully.

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Black Bear
research_grounding.use: Wildlife pressure around camps, food, forests, and travel routes.

image_prompt.subject: a rough frontier map being assembled on a camp blanket with pebbles for towns, cartridges for roads, pine needles for ridges, coffee stains for swampy ground, and a folded county paper partly under the blanket edge
image_prompt.scene: dawn camp beside Clear Creek; saddles nearby, a mule cropped at the edge, smoke low to the ground, boot heels in wet grass, no readable map text
image_prompt.composition: square planning-table image with hands entering frame from three sides; the map is taking shape from humble objects, with the creek direction implied by blue-gray cloth shadow
image_prompt.camera: overhead three-quarter game UI angle, practical and tactile, like an in-engine planning view rather than a clean graphic interface
material_cues: wet leather reins and saddle skirts at frame edge
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Make procedure look like camp craft: quick, dirty, argued over, and good enough to ride before the horses get cold.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine planning-table render
render_traits: modest cloth and leather texture resolution
hard ambient occlusion under hands, stones, cartridges, and blanket folds
dawn light with thin smoke haze
no modern UI panels, grids, or glowing markers
style_modifiers.palette: cold dawn blue, campfire gray, muddy umber, pine green, dull brass, canvas tan, coffee brown, and wet leather black.

text_rendering.instructions: Avoid readable words, labels, grid coordinates, or map text. The map must be object-based and interpretive.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The camp-map objects are legible without readable text.
Every visible prop belongs plausibly to 1899 frontier camp life.
The image feels like a practical map procedure, not a modern UI screen.
No copied game characters, no readable modern text, and no inherited non-western symbols.
